However, I found a plant named Tragacanth, but not specifically "Tragacantha parviceps".

Tragacanth, a type of shrubby plant (Astragalus spp.), is a desert shrub native to the Mediterranean and Middle Eastern regions, its main part of the plant used is the dried sap or gum resin which is used as a thickening agent and for medicinal purposes.

The health benefits of Tragacanth include its use as a natural laxative, to soothe digestive issues, and to help manage coughs and sore throats. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also make it useful for wound healing and skin conditions.

The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include galactomannans, which are complex carbohydrates with potential prebiotic properties, as well as other polysaccharides and polyphenols.

The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include capsules, tablets, and powders, often used to support digestive health and soothe coughs and sore throats.
